title: Initialize project and install dependencies
prompt:
You are contributing to a larger software project using a task plan.
Focus strictly on the described subtask while respecting existing files.
Build upon the code from previous subtasks - the file contents are provided below.
Provide ALL files that should exist after this subtask (including updated versions of existing files).
If tests are needed, include them and mark hasTests=true.
Respond with JSON following the executor schema (project_name, files, notes, hasTests).

Overall project prompt: Create a full, minimal, production-ready landing page for a lab technician. Requirements: single HTML page served by Express; responsive layout; hero with title/subtitle; services list; contact form with client-side validation (no backend, just alert); palette strictly red/black/white; README with run/build/test steps; npm scripts: start, dev, test; tests using Jest to verify hero title and /health endpoint returns ok; no frontend frameworks; use plain CSS and JS.

Context from previously completed subtasks (with file contents):
No subtasks completed yet.

Clarifications (if any):
None provided.

Subtask to execute:
Title: Initialize project and install dependencies
Description: Initialize npm and create the basic structure. Steps: run npm init -y; add directories public/ and tests/; add placeholder files server.js, public/index.html, public/styles.css, public/app.js; create .gitignore for node_modules and logs; install express as a dependency; install jest, supertest, jsdom, and nodemon as devDependencies.
Success criteria: package.json exists with express as dependency and jest, supertest, jsdom, nodemon as devDependencies; public/ and tests/ directories present with placeholder files; npm install completes without errors; .gitignore excludes node_modules.
No explicit dependencies.

IMPORTANT: Include ALL files that should exist, including:
- Modified versions of files from previous subtasks
- New files for this subtask
- Configuration files (package.json, etc.) with updated dependencies if needed

Return only valid JSON with the complete set of files.
